subject: grammar
title: Modals: Probability and Deduction
instruction: Choose the sentence (a or b) which best matches the context on the right.

questions.text: Context: He has a British passport.
Context: I just saw him in the corridor talking to a student.

options.A: Our teacher can't be off sick.
options.B: Our teacher may not be off sick.

questions.text: Context: I advise you to take some warm clothes just in case.

options.A: It can be cold in Delhi in December.
options.B: It must be cold in Delhi in December.

questions.text: Context: He wasn't home when I called at seven.

options.A: John can't have been working late last night.
options.B: John might have been working late last night.

questions.text: Context: He has shares in the most successful company of all time.
Context: It depends if I finish my essay before then.

options.A: I may come to the lecture this afternoon.
options.B: I must come to the lecture this afternoon.

questions.text: Context: Not many people passed it.

options.A: The exam may have been very difficult.
options.B: The exam must have been very difficult.

questions.text: Context: We will have to give him directions.

options.A: John couldn't know how to get here.
options.B: John might not know how to get here.
